<!-- Generated by `make docs-modules` (lib/module-options.nix). Do not edit by hand. -->

## jupiter\.backup\.enable



Replicate this host’s persistent state to the central data store (NAS)\.
The ` stateful ` storage profile defaults this on; appliances/workstations
(impermanent/minimal, or hosts whose data already lives on the NAS over
iSCSI) leave it off\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/backup\.nix



## jupiter\.backup\.datasets

ZFS datasets on this host to replicate to the hub\. Defaulted by the
storage profile (stateful → \[ “rpool/var” ]); extend or override here\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```



*Example:*

```nix
[
  "rpool/var"
]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/backup\.nix



## jupiter\.backups\.paths



List of absolute paths to include in the backup\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/backups\.nix



## jupiter\.backups\.repository



Restic repository location\. Override per-host if the bucket differs\.
S3 credentials are supplied via the restic_env sops secret\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"s3:s3.us-west-004.backblazeb2.com/jupiter-os-backups"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/backups\.nix



## jupiter\.branding\.enable



Whether to enable Enable Jupiter OS / RobCo Pip-Boy branding (MOTD, boot, login)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/branding\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.enable



Whether to enable Enable impermanence (erase your darlings)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.extraDirectories



Host-specific system directories to persist\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.extraFiles



Host-specific system files to persist\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.persistAdminHome



Persist the primary admin account (io)'s home directories\. Turn off on
appliance hosts (e\.g\. kiosks) that have no personal session and only
need to persist a service account’s state via ` users ` below\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.persistPath



The path where persistent state is kept\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"/persist"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.users



Extra per-user home paths to persist (relative to the user’s home),
e\.g\. a kiosk account’s browser profile\. Merged with the admin account
when persistAdminHome is on\.



*Type:*
attribute set of (submodule)



*Default:*

```nix
{ }
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.users\.\<name>\.directories



Directories under this user’s home to persist, relative to the home directory\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.impermanence\.users\.\<name>\.files



Files under this user’s home to persist, relative to the home directory\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/impermanence\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.scheduler\.enable



Whether to enable a sched-ext (scx) userspace CPU scheduler (chaotic-nyx)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/scheduler\.nix



## jupiter\.core\.scheduler\.name



Which sched-ext scheduler to run (services\.scx\.scheduler)\. Pick the
variant suited to the host’s actual workload rather than defaulting
to the gaming-tuned one everywhere, e\.g\. scx_lavd for low-latency
interactive/gaming hosts, scx_bpfland for general desktop
responsiveness, scx_rustland for server/throughput workloads\.
Requires a sched_ext-capable kernel (jupiter\.gaming\.console’s
cachyOsKernel, or any other kernel with CONFIG_SCHED_CLASS_EXT)\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"scx_rustland"
```



*Example:*

```nix
"scx_lavd"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/core/scheduler\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.enable



Whether to enable dual-VT dashboard + Bazzite gaming session with a runtime toggle\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.defaultMode



Which session is foreground at boot\.



*Type:*
one of “dashboard”, “gaming”



*Default:*

```nix
"dashboard"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.gaming\.command



Session command for the gaming VT\. Runs with current-system’s PATH so
` gamescope `/` steam ` resolve to the programs\.\* wrappers\. Defaults to
Steam’s gamepad (Deck) UI inside a gamescope embedded session\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"gamescope --steam -e -- steam -gamepadui"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.gaming\.user



User that owns the Steam install and runs the gaming session\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"gamer"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.gaming\.vt



Virtual terminal the gamescope/Steam session runs on\.



*Type:*
integer between 1 and 12 (both inclusive)



*Default:*

```nix
7
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.homeAssistant\.enable



Whether to enable Home Assistant control via jupiter\.services\.haAgent’s backend-launcher (two switches: Switch to Dashboard / Switch to Gaming)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardGaming\.kiosk\.vt



Virtual terminal the Cage/Chromium dashboard runs on\.



*Type:*
integer between 1 and 12 (both inclusive)



*Default:*

```nix
6
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardKiosk\.enable



Whether to enable TCx Wave Cage + Chromium kiosk session\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-kiosk\.nix



## jupiter\.dashboardKiosk\.url



Home Assistant dashboard URL this unit displays full-screen\.



*Type:*
string



*Example:*

```nix
"https://ha.jupiter.au/kitchen"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/dashboard-kiosk\.nix

Whether to enable Enable desktop environment\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/default\.nix



## jupiter\.desktop\.compositor



Which Wayland compositor to use\.



*Type:*
one of “niri”, “gnome”, “none”



*Default:*

```nix
"niri"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/desktop/default\.nix



## jupiter\.dns\.enable



Whether to enable self-hosted anonymized resolver + internal split-horizon DNS\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/dns\.nix



## jupiter\.dns\.allowedNetworks



Networks permitted to query this resolver\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[
  "127.0.0.0/8"
  "::1/128"
]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/dns\.nix



## jupiter\.dns\.domain



Internal split-horizon zone served authoritatively to LAN clients\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"home.jupiter.au"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/dns\.nix



## jupiter\.dns\.records



Internal FQDN -> IPv4 records (A + PTR) for the split-horizon zone\.



*Type:*
attribute set of string



*Default:*

```nix
{ }
```



*Example:*

```nix
{
  "europa.home.jupiter.au" = "10.1.1.2";
}
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/dns\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.enable



Whether to enable Bazzite-style gaming stack (Jovian gaming mode + chaotic CachyOS)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix

Install OBS Studio + low-overhead Vulkan game capture\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix

Install Vulkan / Mesa diagnostic tools\.

boolean

true

 - modules/gaming/console\.nix

boolean

*Type:*
boolean

## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.apps\.wine



Install Wine/Proton prefix tooling (Bottles, ProtonUp-Qt, protontricks, winetricks)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.cachyOsKernel



Use the CachyOS kernel (linuxPackages_cachyos) from chaotic-nyx\.
Set to false on ZFS-root hosts if the kernel outpaces OpenZFS support,
which would otherwise fail the build\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.decky\.enable



Whether to enable Decky Loader (Steam plugin manager)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.extraPackages



Additional gaming-related packages to install (beyond the app catalogue)\.



*Type:*
list of package



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.gamingMode\.enable



Enable the SteamOS-like gamescope “gaming mode” session (Jovian)\. When
false you still get the full gaming software stack on a normal desktop;
when true the host gains a boot-to-Steam console/handheld experience\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.gamingMode\.autoStart



Boot straight into gaming mode (autologin the session)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.gamingMode\.desktopSession



Desktop session Steam’s ‘Switch to Desktop’ hands off to (null disables the button)\.



*Type:*
null or string



*Default:*

```nix
"niri"/"gnome" inferred from jupiter.desktop.compositor
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.gpu



Primary GPU vendor\. Drives driver and 32-bit graphics setup\.



*Type:*
one of “amd”, “intel”, “nvidia”



*Default:*

```nix
"amd"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.mesaGit



Use bleeding-edge Mesa (chaotic\.mesa-git), matching Bazzite’s shipping Mesa\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.peripherals\.controllers



Broaden game-controller support beyond steam-hardware’s defaults:
Xbox One / Series pads over Bluetooth (xpadneo) and the official
wireless dongle / wired pads (xone), plus a udev rule that stops the
DualSense/DualShock touchpad firing phantom mouse clicks mid-game\.
DualShock / DualSense and Switch Pro pads already work via in-tree
kernel drivers + Bluetooth\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.peripherals\.drawingTablet



Whether to enable OpenTabletDriver support for drawing tablets\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.peripherals\.openrgb



Whether to enable OpenRGB daemon + GUI for RGB peripheral / LED control\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.peripherals\.racingWheels



Sim-racing wheel support: the force-feedback new-lg4ff driver for
Logitech wheels, plus Oversteer to tune them, Solaar for Logitech
device management, and linuxConsoleTools (jstest) for calibration\.
(Fanatec’s hid-fanatec kernel driver is out-of-tree and not vendored
here, unlike GLF-OS which ships it — Oversteer still configures any
supported wheel the kernel exposes\.)



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.steamdeck\.enable



Whether to enable Steam Deck / handheld hardware quirks (Jovian devices\.steamdeck)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.gaming\.console\.user



User that owns the Steam install and auto-logs into gaming mode\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"io"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/gaming/console\.nix



## jupiter\.home\.enable



Whether to enable declarative home-manager environment for user io\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/home/default\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.bond\.enable



Whether to enable 802\.3ad (LACP) link aggregation across the two BCM5720 1GbE ports\.
⚠️  Enable the matching port-aggregation/LACP on the UniFi switch FIRST,
or the NAS will lose network connectivity\.
\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/nas-bond\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.bond\.interfaces



The two physical NICs to aggregate\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[
  "enp2s0f0"
  "enp2s0f1"
]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/nas-bond\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.bond\.mtu



Set to 9000 for jumbo frames (only if switch + clients agree)\.



*Type:*
signed integer



*Default:*

```nix
1500
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/nas-bond\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.iscsi\.enable



Whether to enable LIO iSCSI target exporting zvols to network hosts\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/iscsi\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.iscsi\.luns



Block LUNs to export\. Each maps a zvol to an allowed initiator\.



*Type:*
list of (submodule)



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/iscsi\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.iscsi\.luns\.\*\.dev



Backing device, e\.g\. /dev/zvol/rpool/db\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/iscsi\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.iscsi\.luns\.\*\.initiatorIqn



The CONSUMING host’s initiator IQN (the ACL)\. Read it on that
host from /etc/iscsi/initiatorname\.iscsi\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/iscsi\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.iscsi\.luns\.\*\.name



Backstore name (e\.g\. “db”)\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/iscsi\.nix



## jupiter\.nas\.iscsi\.targetIqn



The NAS’s iSCSI target IQN\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"iqn.2026-06.au.jupiter:europa.target0"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/iscsi\.nix



## jupiter\.pxe\.enable



Whether to enable Pixiecore netboot server for the diskless fleet\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/pxe-server\.nix



## jupiter\.pxe\.cmdLine



Kernel command line\. For a NixOS netboot image this must include
` init=<toplevel>/init ` so the booted kernel finds its closure\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"loglevel=4"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/pxe-server\.nix



## jupiter\.pxe\.initrd



Path (nix store) or URL to the netboot initrd\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/pxe-server\.nix



## jupiter\.pxe\.kernel



Path (nix store) or URL to the netboot kernel (bzImage)\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/network/pxe-server\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.enable



Whether to enable pull-based ZFS replication (syncoid) onto this host\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.interval



systemd OnCalendar interval for the replication timers\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"hourly"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.sources



Datasets to pull onto this host, keyed by an arbitrary name\.



*Type:*
attribute set of (submodule)



*Default:*

```nix
{ }
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.sources\.\<name>\.remote



user@host of the source machine\.



*Type:*
string



*Example:*

```nix
"root@ganymede.home.jupiter.au"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.sources\.\<name>\.sourceDataset



ZFS dataset on the remote host to pull from\.



*Type:*
string



*Example:*

```nix
"rpool/var"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.sources\.\<name>\.targetDataset



ZFS dataset on this host to receive into\.



*Type:*
string



*Example:*

```nix
"tank/backups/ganymede"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.replication\.sshKeyPath



Private SSH key syncoid uses to pull from each remote (typically a sops
secret path)\. The matching public key must be authorized on every source
host with permission to ` zfs send ` the source dataset\.



*Type:*
absolute path

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/replication\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.enable



Whether to enable ha-linux-agent, the Home Assistant companion daemon\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps



Remote-controllable systemd units, exposed as HA switches via
ha-linux-agent’s ` backend-launcher ` (see its ROADMAP\.md “Layer 1 —
session switch”)\. See ` modules/desktop/dashboard-gaming.nix ` for the
canonical consumer (kiosk/gaming VT switch on the TCx Wave units)\.



*Type:*
list of (submodule)



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps\.\*\.group



Profiles sharing a group are mutually exclusive\.



*Type:*
null or string



*Default:*

```nix
null
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps\.\*\.icon



Optional mdi icon override\.



*Type:*
null or string



*Default:*

```nix
null
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps\.\*\.id



Stable id — becomes the MQTT switch entity id and command-topic allowlist entry\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps\.\*\.name



HA-facing display name\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps\.\*\.scope



` systemctl --user ` vs plain ` systemctl `\.



*Type:*
one of “user”, “system”



*Default:*

```nix
"user"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.launcherApps\.\*\.unit



systemd unit this profile starts/stops\.



*Type:*
string

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.haAgent\.mqttHost



Mosquitto broker to publish sensors/commands to\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"ganymede.home.jupiter.au"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/ha-agent\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.loki\.enable



Whether to enable Loki log aggregation + syslog receiver\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/loki\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.loki\.dataDir



Persistent storage directory for Loki chunks + index\.



*Type:*
absolute path



*Default:*

```nix
"/var/lib/loki"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/loki\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.loki\.httpPort



HTTP port Loki’s query/push API listens on\.



*Type:*
16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)



*Default:*

```nix
3100
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/loki\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.loki\.syslogPort



TCP port the syslog receiver listens on (RFC5424)\.



*Type:*
16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)



*Default:*

```nix
514
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/loki\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.enable



Whether to enable Mosquitto MQTT broker\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.allowAnonymous



Allow unauthenticated clients\. Defaults to true only while no users are
defined, for quick bring-up on a trusted VLAN\. Define ` users ` (with sops
password files) to require authentication\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

````nix
true when no `users` are defined
````

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.openFirewall



Open the broker port in the firewall (intended for the trusted LAN)\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.port



TCP port the broker listens on\.



*Type:*
16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)



*Default:*

```nix
1883
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.users



MQTT users\. Each ` passwordFile ` holds the plaintext password (e\.g\. a
sops secret)\. ` acl ` grants topic access — mosquitto’s acl-file
plugin (which the underlying ` services.mosquitto ` module always
loads) default-denies any topic with no matching rule, so a user
with an empty ` acl ` can authenticate but can’t publish or subscribe
to anything\. Always set ` acl ` for a user that needs to do anything\.



*Type:*
attribute set of (submodule)



*Default:*

```nix
{ }
```



*Example:*

```nix
{
  homeassistant = {
    passwordFile = config.sops.secrets.mqtt_homeassistant.path;
    acl = [ "readwrite #" ];
  };
  ha-linux-agent = {
    passwordFile = config.sops.secrets.mqtt_ha_linux_agent.path;
    acl = [
      "readwrite homeassistant/#"
      "readwrite ha-linux-agent/#"
    ];
  };
}

```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.users\.\<name>\.acl



Topic ACL entries, each ` "[read|write|readwrite] <topic pattern>" `
(mosquitto acl-file syntax — see ` man mosquitto.conf `)\. Required:
an authenticated user with no ` acl ` entries is denied every
topic, not granted full access\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.mqtt\.users\.\<name>\.passwordFile



File containing the user’s plaintext password\.



*Type:*
absolute path

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/mqtt\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.n8n\.database\.enable



Whether to enable back n8n with PostgreSQL instead of the bundled SQLite\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/n8n\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.n8n\.database\.host



PostgreSQL host n8n connects to\.



*Type:*
string



*Example:*

```nix
"callisto.home.jupiter.au"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/n8n\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.n8n\.database\.name



Database name n8n connects to\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"n8n"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/n8n\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.n8n\.database\.passwordFile



Path to the DB password (a sops secret readable by the n8n user)\. Passed
to n8n via DB_POSTGRESDB_PASSWORD_FILE, so it’s never in the Nix store\.



*Type:*
absolute path

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/n8n\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.n8n\.database\.port

PostgreSQL port n8n connects to\.



*Type:*
16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)



*Default:*

```nix
5432
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/n8n\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.n8n\.database\.user



Database role n8n authenticates as\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"n8n"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/n8n\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.postgresql\.enable



Whether to enable PostgreSQL server\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/postgresql\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.postgresql\.package



PostgreSQL package/version\.



*Type:*
package



*Default:*

```nix
"pkgs.postgresql_16"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/postgresql\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.postgresql\.dataDir



Data directory root (a versioned subdir is created beneath it)\.



*Type:*
absolute path



*Default:*

```nix
"/var/lib/postgresql"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/postgresql\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.postgresql\.databases



Databases to provision, keyed by name\. Each gets a login role of the same
name owning a database of the same name, with its password set from
passwordFile and network access from allowedClients\.



*Type:*
attribute set of (submodule)



*Default:*

```nix
{ }
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/postgresql\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.postgresql\.databases\.\<name>\.allowedClients



CIDRs allowed to connect to this database over TCP (scram-sha-256)\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```



*Example:*

```nix
[
  "10.1.1.20/32"
]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/postgresql\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.postgresql\.databases\.\<name>\.passwordFile



sops secret holding this role’s password (readable by the postgres user)\.



*Type:*
absolute path

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/postgresql\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.stateBackup\.enable



Whether to enable periodic logical backup of local service state to a spool directory\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/state-backup\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.stateBackup\.interval



systemd OnCalendar interval\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"hourly"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/state-backup\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.stateBackup\.keep



How many timestamped postgres dumps to retain in the spool\.



*Type:*
signed integer



*Default:*

```nix
24
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/state-backup\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.stateBackup\.postgres



Include an hourly pg_dumpall of the local PostgreSQL\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/state-backup\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.stateBackup\.rsyncPaths



Directories to mirror (rsync --delete) into the spool\.



*Type:*
list of string



*Default:*

```nix
[ ]
```



*Example:*

```nix
[
  "/var/lib/loki"
]
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/state-backup\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.stateBackup\.spoolDir



Directory to write backups into\. MUST be on central, backed-up storage (e\.g\. an NFS mount of the NAS)\.



*Type:*
absolute path

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/state-backup\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.syncthing\.enable



Whether to enable Enable Syncthing for user io\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/syncthing\.nix



## jupiter\.services\.syncthing\.dataDir



Base directory for Syncthing’s data, config/index, and the default
location for new folders\. On personal machines this is io’s home
(/home/io)\. On the NAS hub set it to a path on protected storage
(e\.g\. /tank/personal) so the canonical synced copy is redundant,
snapshotted, and in the offsite path — not stranded on the OS disk\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"/home/io"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/services/syncthing\.nix



## jupiter\.storage\.disk



The OS disk to partition\. disko is DESTRUCTIVE to this device, so it
defaults to a placeholder that fails the assertion below — set it to
the real /dev/disk/by-id path before installing\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"/dev/disk/by-id/REPLACE-ME"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/zfs-profiles\.nix



## jupiter\.storage\.espSize



Size of the EFI System Partition\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"1G"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/zfs-profiles\.nix



## jupiter\.storage\.profile



Opinionated ZFS-on-root disko layout for this host\. “none” leaves the
host to declare its own layout (or be diskless)\. See the profile
descriptions at the top of modules/storage/zfs-profiles\.nix\.



*Type:*
one of “none”, “impermanent”, “stateful”, “minimal”



*Default:*

```nix
"none"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/zfs-profiles\.nix



## jupiter\.storage\.smartMonitoring\.enable



Whether to enable S\.M\.A\.R\.T\. self-tests and health checks on all attached disks\.



*Type:*
boolean



*Default:*

```nix
false
```



*Example:*

```nix
true
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/smart-monitoring\.nix



## jupiter\.storage\.smartMonitoring\.checkInterval



Seconds between smartd health checks\.



*Type:*
signed integer



*Default:*

```nix
1800
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/smart-monitoring\.nix



## jupiter\.storage\.smartMonitoring\.selfTestSchedule



smartd ` -s ` schedule: short self-test daily at 02:00, long self-test
weekly on Sundays at 03:00\. Passed straight through to smartd\.conf\.



*Type:*
string



*Default:*

```nix
"-s (S/../.././02|L/../../7/03)"
```

*Declared by:*
 - modules/storage/smart-monitoring\.nix
